title: A New Archive of Apollo's Lunar Seismic Data
authors: C. Nunn; Y. Nakamura; S. Kedar; M. P. Panning
main_result: 96.5 percent valid records reported across mission coverage
novelty_effect: supports reproducibility contribution but not ML novelty

title: New identification of deep moonquakes in the Apollo lunar seismic data
venue: Physics of the Earth and Planetary Interiors
main_result: increased identified deep events from 1360 to 7245 and revised nests
novelty_effect: eliminates any discovery-first claim and mandates family-disjoint splits

title: New events discovered in the Apollo lunar seismic data
authors: R. C. Bulow; C. L. Johnson; P. M. Shearer
event_classes: deep moonquake A1 and catalog grades
split_strategy: known family stacks against continuous data
main_result: found 123 additional A1 events beyond 323 cataloged events
overlap_with_lunaseis: grade definitions and family leakage controls
novelty_effect: weakens generic detection novelty; supports rigorous grouping

title: Identification of new events in Apollo 16 lunar seismic data by Hidden Markov Model-based event detection and classification
split_strategy: time-based continuous analysis at one station
false_alarm_metric: false alarms discussed; daily adaptation reduced them
main_result: demonstrated single-station automated detection/classification and new candidates

title: Detecting moonquakes using convolutional neural networks a non-local training set and transfer learning
authors: F. Civilini; R. C. Weber; Z. Jiang; D. Phillips; W. D. Pan
dataset: Earth PFO training; Apollo PSE and Apollo 17 LSPE testing
cross_station_evaluation: per-station results but not station-held-out lunar training
negative_sampling: Earth noise windows; lunar continuous hours
code_availability: article supplements/catalog; code status not fully audited
main_result: 89-96 percent station accuracy averages on 200 Grade-A PSE events; telemetry motivation
limitations: 300 s tolerance; no lunar training split; limited false-alarm ground truth
overlap_with_lunaseis: direct ML telemetry and lightweight CNN predecessor

title: Thermal Moonquake Characterization and Cataloging Using Frequency-Based Algorithms and Stochastic Gradient Descent
authors: F. Civilini; R. C. Weber; A. Husker
novelty_effect: does not directly block PSE station-generalization contribution

title: New Views of Lunar Seismicity Brought by Analysis of Newly Discovered Moonquakes in Apollo Short-Period Seismic Data
code_availability: associated catalog reported; location to audit
main_result: expanded shallow moonquakes from 28 to 74 and found more than 22000 events
limitations: not integrated into 2019 PDS expanded catalog
overlap_with_lunaseis: changes label completeness and class viability
novelty_effect: requires newer-catalog reconciliation before final labels

title: Newly Discovered Temperature-Related Long-Period Signals in Lunar Seismic Data by Deep Learning
authors: X. Liu; Z. Xiao; J. Li; Y. Nakamura
stations: at least 3 of 4 long-running PSE stations
main_result: found nightly temperature-related long-period signals likely instrument/heater induced
novelty_effect: strengthens artifact controls and weakens naive catalog-negative assumption

title: Fourier Neural Operator for Moonquake Detection
dataset: Earth PFO; Apollo PSE; Apollo 17 LSPE
split_strategy: stratified train/validation/test in Earth-only and Earth plus 64 moonquake regimes
metrics: F1 and efficiency metrics; exact table extraction pending
false_alarm_metric: reports few false alarms but operational rate extraction pending
main_result: FNO supports variable lengths/rates with fewer parameters and peak lunar F1 roughly 0.75-0.85 in Earth-trained regimes
limitations: small lunar sample; balanced windows; exact leakage/station protocol needs audit
novelty_effect: materially weakens original candidate novelty; strict LOSO/family/calibration/operational study may remain

title: Discovery of Repeating Shallow Moonquakes in the Apollo Lunar Seismic Data
overlap_with_lunaseis: extends repeating-family risk beyond deep events
novelty_effect: requires similarity/duplicate checks for shallow events too
